markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define schema version migrations and compatibility handling for persisted Tiptap/ProseMirror JSON. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: TiptapSchemaVersioning leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TiptapSchemaVersioning is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 5f1b0939: schema_versioning.test.ts 5/5 - assertEditorSchema pass-through/migrate/refuse-newer/typed-unknown wired into RichDocumentView load. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: H2 schema fail-open (newer/unknown-schema docs editable + saveable; one save persists the ProseMirror-stripped doc)
evidence.fix: RichDocumentView fail-closed: schemaBlocked state -> editor mounted READ-ONLY (data-schema-blocked), Save disabled, onSave refuses with typed schemaSaveBlockedError; schemaMismatchError hint states the read-only/save-blocked contract
tests: RichDocumentView.test.tsx: fails CLOSED on a newer-schema document: read-only editor, save blocked, typed banner (incl. destructive-save negative: forced edit + Save click never reaches saveRichDocument)
backend_error.test.ts: builds a typed save-blocked error for schema-blocked documents

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: schema_versioning.test.ts 5/5 PASS in current tree (within vitest 62/468)

findings.title: Fail-open on schema mismatch: newer/unknown-schema documents stay editable and saveable -> silent data loss
findings.evidence: RichDocumentView.tsx:74-79 - when assertEditorSchema returns ok:false the view STILL setEditorContent(response.document.content_json) and leaves the editor editable with Save enabled. schema_versioning.ts:54-57 states a non-ok result "must be surfaced (MT-174) rather than loaded". ProseMirror (enableContentCheck default false) silently DROPS unknown node types on parse, so one Save persists a stripped document over the newer-schema head (versions table keeps history, head is degraded).
findings.fix: On ok:false: keep the typed banner, render read-only (editable:false) or a raw-JSON preview, and hard-disable Save until reload under a compatible editor. Add a regression test: load newer_than_editor doc -> editor readOnly -> save blocked.

findings.title: Migrated content is never persisted until a manual edit; migration ladder unexercised end-to-end
findings.evidence: RichDocumentView.tsx:74-75 loads assertion.content for compatibility "migrated" but isDirty stays false, so the migrated JSON + new schema_version are not saved; every load re-migrates. SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS is empty (schema_versioning.ts:46) and no integration test covers migrate->save->reload stamping.
findings.fix: After a successful migration either mark dirty with an explicit "migrated - save to upgrade" notice or auto-save a migration version; add an end-to-end test with a synthetic v0->v1 migration through RichDocumentView.
findings.residual: auto-persisting migrated content is a WRITE the operator did not request - it needs an explicit auto-migration-save policy decision (and the migration ladder is still empty: no production migration exists). The migration walk itself is unit-proven; H2 fail-closed (6e48aa6b) guarantees a mismatched doc can never be silently overwritten meanwhile. Revisit when the first real schema migration is registered.

parity_gaps: ED-CORE-004 schema versioning: module PRESENT, consumer fail-open -> PARTIAL at the surface
missing_tests: load newer_than_editor schema -> editor is read-only and save is blocked
load unknown_version schema -> unknown nodes are NOT silently dropped into a saveable doc
migrated document -> save stamps current schema_version (end-to-end through RichDocumentView)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-162 requires editor-side schema-version assertion + forward migration + typed compatibility handling for persisted Tiptap/ProseMirror JSON, wired into the load path. The code (app/src/lib/editor/schema_versioning.ts + RichDocumentView wiring) implements all four cases, aligns with Master Spec 7.1.1.8 ("deterministic save/load/round-trip ... across schema migration"), and the prior adversarial HIGH fail-open data-loss finding is genuinely fixed (fail-closed read-only + save-blocked) with a real destructive-save negative test.
findings: schema_versioning.ts:105-170 — assertEditorSchema implements all four compatibility outcomes: current pass-through (110-119), forward-migration walk with a cycle guard (122-144), newer-than-editor typed refusal via versionRank comparison without down-conversion (147-159), and typed unknown_version mismatch (161-169). Pure, deterministic, no network — matches the MT's required behavior.

RichDocumentView.tsx:464-496 — the guard is wired into the REAL load path: reload() runs assertEditorSchema over response.document.schema_version + content_json from the live /knowledge/documents backend. Not isolated/dead code. ok:true sets editor content; ok:false sets schemaBlocked + typed schemaMismatchError banner.

Prior adversarial HIGH (fail-open: newer/unknown-schema docs stayed editable+saveable -> ProseMirror silently drops unknown nodes -> one save overwrites authority) is genuinely FIXED in commit 6e48aa6b: editor mounts read-only (RichDocumentView.tsx:914 readOnly={schemaBlocked!==null}), Save disabled (880), and onSave refuses any path with typed schemaSaveBlockedError defense-in-depth (598-609).

RichDocumentView.test.tsx:1002-1044 — destructive-save negative test: load rich_document_v999 -> data-schema-blocked=true, editor data-readonly=true, schema-kind banner, and forced edit + Save click asserts saveRichDocument call count is unchanged (the stripped doc can never reach the backend authority).

schema_versioning.ts:46 SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS is empty, which is CORRECT (rich_document_v1 is the first schema; no production migration exists yet). The forward-migration walk is not a placeholder escape — it is exercised end-to-end by a synthetic v0->current chain (schema_versioning.test.ts:52-82) proving the mechanics before a real migration ships. No todo!/unimplemented/pending-MT/*Unavailable escape reachable from the load path.

backend RichDocument.schema_version is real and backend-stamped (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_documents.rs); the editor module is pure client reconciliation over that real field — PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority unchanged, consistent with the MT constraint that the backend stays canonical.

Aside (non-blocking): the adversarial MEDIUM 'migrated content never auto-persisted' is honestly DEFERRED — auto-saving a migration is an unrequested write and the empty ladder means no production migration is unexercised; fail-closed guard prevents any silent overwrite meanwhile. Not a defect.

Aside (paperwork, non-blocking per ground rules): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; same section numbers resolve and 7.1.1.8 carries the schema-migration requirement in both.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
